title: Write a variable-length bit value to the DEFLATE bit buffer
signature: pub fn send_bits(state: &mut DeflateState, value: u16, length: u8)
notes: Appends `length` bits of `value` to state.bi_buf (16-bit buffer). When the buffer fills, flushes two bytes into state.pending in LSB-first order (RFC 1951 §3.1.1). Requires DeflateState fields: bi_buf: u16, bi_valid: u8, pending: Vec<u8>.

title: Flush the remaining bits in the DEFLATE bit buffer, padding with zeros
notes: Flushes state.bi_buf to state.pending, writing 1 or 2 bytes as needed based on state.bi_valid. Resets bi_buf and bi_valid to zero.

title: Flush complete bytes from the DEFLATE bit buffer to pending output
notes: If the bit buffer contains 16+ bits, emit 2 bytes and clear it. If 8+ bits, emit 1 byte and shift. Otherwise do nothing. Called periodically during encoding to keep bi_buf from overflowing.

title: Assign canonical Huffman codes given bit lengths
signature: pub fn gen_codes(tree: &mut [HuffmanNode], max_code: usize, bl_count: &[u16])
notes: Canonical Huffman code assignment per RFC 1951 §3.2.2. For each symbol, sets tree[i].code to the reflected (LSB-first) canonical code whose bit length is tree[i].len. Uses bl_count[len] = number of codes of that length to derive next_code[len]. Symbols with len==0 are skipped.
